beats.activity: Wake up, make espresso, review the day ahead while the rain patters outside.
beats.reasoning: Evelyn's ritual—espresso and quiet morning time. The drizzle fits her mood; she's risk-averse and will check traffic conditions before heading to Bellevue.

beats.activity: Commute to Bellevue bakery, taking I-5 south to avoid the SR-520 closure; rain slows traffic slightly.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Evelyn leaves early to beat rush and avoids the bridge entirely by routing I-5→I-90. Conservative timing for wet roads.

beats.activity: Prep croissant dough, laminate, shape, and oversee the morning bake. Steady bakery rhythm.
beats.reasoning: Evelyn is a pastry chef; early arrival lets her set the tone for the day. Rain outside won't dampen the warm bakery. No soccer match today—just the steady work she loves.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a quick sandwich and coffee at a nearby café in Bellevue Downtown.
beats.reasoning: Light meal during rain; staying local rather than driving in drizzle. Keeps her fueled for the afternoon bake.

beats.activity: Return to bakery, finish afternoon service and bake. Rain tapers off by 2pm; steady work through closing.
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon shift. Rain ends by 2pm, so commute home will be damp but passable. Evelyn settles into her work rhythm.

beats.activity: Drive home from Bellevue via I-90 and I-5, rain cleared, roads drying.
beats.reasoning: Reverse commute is lighter than morning. Rain has passed; roads are clear enough for normal evening drive home.

beats.activity: Stop at QFC to pick up fresh herbs, bread flour, and vegetables for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Evelyn is gregarious but values close friends over crowds; a quick neighborhood errand fits her routine. She's planning a camping trip and likes fresh ingredients.

beats.activity: Prepare a simple dinner at home: sauté vegetables and herbs, pair with a home-brewed beer.
beats.reasoning: Evelyn unwinds at home with the things she loves—fresh food, her own beer, and solitude after a full work day. No big crowds, just her space.

beats.activity: Relax at home, review camping gear and finalize plans for the weekend trip. Maybe read or listen to music.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down at home before bed. She has something to look forward to (camping trip), and this quiet evening lets her mentally prepare and recharge after a rainy Tuesday.
Full night sleep (7.5 hours), ending before 6am Wednesday. Evelyn needs rest after a full work day; no late-night energy spikes.

diary: The rain started before my alarm even went off—I could hear it drumming against the window while I pulled my espresso shot. Grey morning, the kind that makes you grateful for hot coffee. I-5 was my escape route since everyone else was probably cursing the SR-520 closure; the detour worked out fine, just slower than usual. At the bakery, I lost myself in the lamination work—there's something meditative about those folds, the precision of it. Grabbed lunch at Cesura with my hands still dusted in flour. By afternoon, the rain had mostly cleared and the work felt lighter somehow. Stopped at QFC on the way home, picked up fresh dill and some decent bread flour. Made a simple dinner, nothing fancy—just vegetables, herbs, and one of my homebrews. Spent the evening sprawled on the couch with my camping checklist, imagining the mountains already. Felt peaceful tonight.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure could've derailed the day, but I've been doing this long enough to anticipate Seattle traffic chaos. I-5 felt like the safe bet, and it was. Work was grounding—those repetitive motions with the dough keep my mind settled. I noticed I wasn't rushing today; maybe it's because the weather made everything feel slower anyway. By afternoon I was in a better mood than I'd started, which surprised me. The camping trip is real now—not just a someday thing—and that shifted something in me. I wanted to spend the evening planning rather than scrolling, so I did. Small choice, but it mattered.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
